finding: `node atm.mjs git commit --dry-run` and the real pre-commit hook can disagree about active foreign governance residue ownership.
expectedBehavior: The same ownership proof rules must be shared by the commit bundle builder and pre-commit hook: active direction lock or active ledger claim should protect non-staged foreign governance residue from blocking another active task, while orphan residue stays fail-closed.
evidenceOrRepro: During TASK-AAO-FABLE-005 and TASK-AAO-0188, both task-scoped dry-runs became green only after the wrapper was patched, but the real pre-commit still found additional direction-lock scope drift for protected override audit files. FABLE-005 delivery commit `6a535795` and 0188 delivery commit `bb97753b` required extra scope amendments before real commits could pass.
followUp: Fold wrapper/hook residue ownership into a shared helper under `TASK-GIT-0013`; add regression for dry-run/write parity with two active claims and foreign bundle manifests.
